data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Rebound on Soaring Rate Cut Odds as BLS Cancels October CPI Report
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed in the green on Friday after comments from Fed Bank of New York President John Williams, a close confidant of Fed Chair Jerome Powell, revived rate cut odds at the December 9-10 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Williams said that interest rates are "moderately restrictive" and signaled support for lower rates to lift up the labor market. Lower rates benefit the labor market by making it cheaper to borrow capital, stimulating economic activity and employment.

"Therefore, I still see room for a further adjustment in the near term to the target range for the federal funds rate to move the stance of policy closer to the range of neutral, thereby maintaining the balance between the achievement of our two goals," said Williams.

Bianco Research founder Jim Bianco notes that of the 12 voting FOMC members, five have expressed support for a rate cut, while five have called for an unchanged rate. The opinions of the remaining two members, Powell and Fed Vice Chair Philip Jefferson, are still unclear.

The odds of a 25 bps reduction next month surged following his comments, rising to 69.5% compared to 39.1% a day ago and 44.4% a week ago on CME's FedWatch tool.

The Fed will have to make its decision without a key inflation indicator, as the Bureau of Labor Statistics announced that October's Consumer Price Index (CPI) would not be released due to data collection disruptions caused by the government shutdown. November's CPI will be published after the December FOMC, on December 18.

Lower rates could potentially boost consumer sentiment, which remains deflated. The University of Michigan's November Index of Consumer Sentiment tallied at 51, which was above the estimate of 50.3 but down from 53.6 in October. For context, a reading above 70 indicates optimism, while a reading below 50 indicates pessimism.

"After the federal shutdown ended, sentiment lifted slightly from its mid-month reading," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu. "However, consumers remain frustrated about the persistence of high prices and weakening incomes." At the same time, inflation expectations are still falling. Consumers expect prices to rise by 4.5% over the 12 months, down from 4.6%. In addition, long-term inflation expectations dropped to 3.4% from 3.9%.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.98% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.77%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BigBear.ai Stock Forecast: What Do Analysts Expect After 120% Rally?
data.description:
BigBear.ai (BBAI) builds AI tools for defense and national security. The stock is up about 120% over the past year, helped by stronger-than-expected Q3 numbers and rising interest in AI used across military and government programs. More recently, the stock has pulled back about 18% over the past month due to broader market weakness and valuation concerns. Overall, Wall Street is cautiously optimistic about BigBear.ai stock, with the average price target implying an 21% upside potential.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

For context, BigBear.ai's Q3 revenue fell 20% to $33.1 million, but surpassed analyst estimates of $31.81 million. The company also reported a net loss of $0.03 per share, better than the expected loss of $0.07 per share. Along with its earnings, BigBear.ai announced plans to buy Ask Sage, a generative AI platform for secure defense and national security use, in a $250 million deal.

Analysts' Views on BBAI Stock

After the earnings release, H.C. Wainwright's analyst Scott Buck maintained his Buy rating on BigBear.ai, pointing to the Ask Sage acquisition as the key reason for his positive stance. He believes the deal will support growth in defense and national security work, improve margins, and add recurring revenue.

Buck noted that while Q3 results were mixed, the company still holds solid cash reserves, giving room for future investment. He kept his $8 price target and said the Ask Sage deal could play a meaningful role in improving results by 2026, even as short-term risks remain.

Meanwhile, investor Chris Neiger remains cautious on BigBear.ai stock. He pointed to the company's declining revenue and slower growth. Neiger highlighted that BigBear.ai had already cut its 2025 revenue outlook to $132.5 million from $170 million after missing earlier guidance. He also noted rising losses, with an adjusted loss of $8.5 million, more than double the prior year.

Despite those challenges, Neiger noted the stock was trading at a premium valuation, with a 13x price-to-sales ratio compared to 3.5x for the S&P 500 (SPX). In his view, much of the optimism was already reflected in the price, and slower growth could lead to a sharp pullback.

Is BBAI a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on BBAI stock, based on one Buy and one Hold assigned in the last three months. The average BigBear.ai share price target is $6.50, which implies upside potential of 20.37% from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Gold to Surge to $4,500 by Q2 2026, Says UBS
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is up by 54% this year and is on track for its best year since 1979. UBS expects the strong performance to extend into 2026.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The firm now expects the precious metal to reach $4,500, up from its previous target of $4,200 by the second quarter of 2026, implying upside of 10% from current levels. UBS also raised its upside target to $4,900 from $4,700, implying a 20% rise, while maintaining its downside target of $3,700.

Lower Rates and Geopolitical Uncertainty Benefit Gold, Says UBS

UBS points out that the Fed is likely to continue its rate-cutting cycle in 2026. That benefits gold because lower interest rates reduce the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding assets. Furthermore, investors generally flock to gold, a safe-haven asset, during times of uncertainty.

"We expect gold demand to rise further in 2026, influenced by anticipated Fed rate cuts, lower real yields, continued geopolitical uncertainties, and changes in the domestic US policy environment," said the firm.

data.title: Cathie Wood Loads Up on CoreWeave (CRWV), and Crypto Stocks; Trims Stakes in GitLab, AMD, and Other Tech Stocks
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) reported new trades on Friday, November 10, according to daily fund disclosures. ARK bought shares of AI cloud company CoreWeave (CRWV), gene-editing firm CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP), and crypto names such as Bullish (BLSH), BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), and the ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (ARKB).

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

At the same time, the fund sold more shares of software maker GitLab (GTLB), along with cuts in Exact Sciences (EXAS), satellite network firm Iridium (IRDM), and chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD).

ARK Buys CoreWeave, Crypto, and CRISPR Stocks

ARK added to several key positions on Friday. The biggest buy was 350,734 shares of CoreWeave, worth about $24.27 million, showing strong faith in the AI cloud infrastructure company. This adds to ARK's recent pattern of buying more CoreWeave. Notably, the stock fell almost 22% over the past five sessions after the company lowered its 2025 revenue outlook during its latest earnings call. Wood now appears to be buying the dip.

Along with CoreWeave, ARK kept building its crypto exposure. It bought 49,900 shares of Bullish, 31,890 shares of BitMine, and 20,700 shares of the ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF. These buys come as Bitcoin (BTC) fell below $85,000 on Friday, extending the recent pullback. This marks its lowest level since April, when it briefly slipped below $75,000.

ARK also increased its stake in biotech. The firm purchased 67,078 shares of CRISPR for about $3.3 million, keeping its focus on gene-editing and next-generation medical research.

ARK Cuts GitLab Again, Along With EXAS, AMD, and IRDM

On the selling side, ARK continued trimming several positions. The biggest move was the sale of 670,144 shares of GitLab, worth about $28.49 million.

The fund also sold 227,153 shares of Exact Sciences, valued at roughly $22.87 million, and cut 169,485 shares of Iridium, totaling about $2.68 million. In addition, ARK made a smaller trim in AMD, selling 1,623 shares worth about $334,370.

data.title: With Archer Aviation Stock (ACHR) Down 26% YTD, Let's Look at Who Owns It
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR) stock has had a tough run, with the stock down 34% over the past month and 26% year-to-date. The drop comes despite steady progress in flight testing, new partnership deals, and better-than-expected Q3 results. The main concern remains that Archer still does not generate meaningful revenue, and the market may be losing patience. The stock has also been weighed down by the ongoing FAA approval process.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Selling pressure also increased after Archer filed a prospectus on November 19 for the resale of more than 1 million shares, which raised dilution worries. Even with these near-term challenges, most analysts remain optimistic. For instance, Canaccord recently lifted its price target to $13 from $12 and kept a Buy rating, noting that Archer's move to license its battery-electric powertrain to a third party could create a new revenue stream. With all of this in view, it's a good time to look at who owns ACHR shares.

Now, according to TipRanks' ownership page, public companies and individual investors own 45% of ACHR. They are followed by insiders, ETFs, mutual funds, and other institutional investors, at 24.28%, 20.44%, 8.29%, and 1.99%, respectively.

Digging Deeper into ACHR's Ownership Structure

Looking closely at top shareholders, Stellantis N.V. owns the highest stake in ACHR at 9.29%. Following that is Marc E. Lore, who owns about 8.70% of the company.

Among the top ETF holders, the Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK) owns a 3.02% stake in Archer Aviation stock, followed by the SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR), with a 2.73% stake.

Moving to mutual funds, Vanguard Index Funds hold about 4.63% of ACHR. Meanwhile, Fidelity Salem Street Trust owns 0.91% of the company.

Is Archer Aviation Stock a Good Buy?

Despite the stock's steep decline in 2025, the Street's analysts remain optimistic about the company's prospects. Based on four Buys and one Hold rating, Archer Aviation boasts a "Strong Buy" consensus with an average ACHR stock price target of $12.40. This implies a 72.70% upside from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Opendoor Stock (OPEN) Surged 10% — and Can the Rally Continue?
data.description:
Opendoor Technologies (OPEN), the online real estate company, jumped on Friday after a new filing showed that hedge fund DE Shaw owns a 6.4% stake in the company as of November 13. The stock closed at $6.75, up 9.58% on the day, and is now up more than 325% in 2025.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The reaction echoed a move seen in late September, when Jane Street revealed a 5.9% stake and the stock jumped more than 7%. With both Jane Street and now DE Shaw on the shareholder list, some traders believe more large firms are starting to show interest in the company. That sentiment likely helped support Friday's move.

Rate-Cut Hopes Also Helped the Stock

Another factor driving the stock higher was improving sentiment around interest rates. After comments from New York Fed President John Williams, traders began pricing in a higher chance of a potential rate cut in December.

Lower mortgage rates make home buying more affordable and can help restart demand in the housing market. For Opendoor, any pickup in buyer activity could mean more sales and better pricing. Since the company relies on steady home sales, even a small improvement in borrowing costs can make a meaningful difference.

Shaw's Stake May Not Signal a Long-Term Bullish Call

Even though the DE Shaw filing sparked excitement, the details suggest the move may be more of a short-term trade than a long-term bet. Most of those shares are held under DE Shaw Valence Portfolios, a fund that focuses on quick, trading-based strategies rather than long-term investing. So the filing alone doesn't confirm that DE Shaw believes in Opendoor's long-term future.

One likely reason for the timing is Opendoor's recent special warrant dividend. To receive those warrants, investors had to hold the stock on November 18. For a fund like DE Shaw Valence, the value may come from the warrants, not the stock itself. These warrants can be traded or used with listed options to take advantage of short-term price gaps.

In that case, the stake looks more like a short-term trade than a signal of long-term confidence.

Is OPEN Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts remain cautious about Opendoor's outlook. Wall Street analysts have a Hold consensus rating on OPEN stock based on one Buy, two Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average OPEN price target of $4.35 per share implies 35.56% downside risk.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Is Li Auto a Buy Ahead of Q3? What the Numbers Signal
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker Li Auto (LI) will report its third-quarter results before the market opens on Wednesday, November 26. The stock has struggled this year and is down about 25% so far, mainly because deliveries have slowed and competition in China's EV market has grown stronger. October deliveries showed the pressure clearly. The company delivered 31,767 vehicles, which was down 38.3% from a year ago and 6.4% lower than September.

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Meanwhile, demand for its extended-range models has softened, and that remains a major challenge. While Li Auto has launched new SUVs and cut prices to stay competitive, those moves have not yet led to a steady rebound in sales. As a result, Wall Street analysts remain cautious heading into Q3 results.

What to Expect from Li Auto's Q3 Earnings 

Wall Street analysts expect Li Auto to post $0.10 per share in earnings for Q3, down sharply from $0.51 last year. Revenue is also forecast to decline. According to TipRanks estimates, Wall Street models $3.71 billion in sales versus $5.98 billion a year ago.
